scenario: An Activity has two outgoing sequence flows, each with its own conditionExpression. No explicit Gateway. Team wonders if this is valid BPMN.
question: Implicit Gateway from Activity with conditional flows?

options.text: md:116 (verbatim: "you could've left out this inclusive gateway by drawing two outgoing sequence flows directly out of the preceding task ...

Both conditional flows fire simultaneously when conditions are true -- this mischaracterizes the semantics. In Camunda 8 the implicit gateway behavior from an activity evaluates conditions in sequence order. Explicit gateways provide clearer semantics for such use cases.

0 specification explicitly permits conditional sequence flows on activities, and Camunda 8 acknowledges this in best-practice documentation. While the engine supports it, Camunda recommends using an explicit gateway instead for readability, because process readers understand gateway flow semantics better than implicit conditional flows on activities.

a.text: Incorrect. Implicit decision from Activity: BPMN spec: Activities can have multiple outgoing flows with conditions. Engine evaluates conditions; routes per truth. Implicit XOR semantic (or implicit conditional). Typical behavior: Conditions evaluated: at Activity completion. First true branch activates (FIRST-like). Default flow marker (slash): activates if no condition true. Without default flow + no condition true: typically Incident. Visual: ` [Activity A] ──cond1──> [B] ──cond2──> [C] ──default─> [D] (with slash marker) ` Compared to explicit Gateway: Implicit (Activity with conditional flows): Pros: compact diagram. Cons: less visually explicit; readers might miss decision logic. Explicit XOR Gateway: Pros: clear decision point; standard pattern. Cons: extra element. Best practice: Use explicit Gateway: clearer intent. Especially for non-trivial decisions. Camunda 8 support: Verify per version: Implicit conditional flows: typically supported. Engine behavior matches spec. Use cases: Simple two-way branch: condition + default. Tight diagram: avoid extra Gateway. For multiple-condition complex: Use explicit Gateway always. For Inclusive (OR) semantic with implicit: Some interpretations: all true branches fire (OR-like). Verify Camunda 8 behavior; typically XOR-like. Linting (per Set 14 Q59): Rules may enforce explicit Gateways. Org convention. Anti-patterns: Implicit decision for complex logic: hard to read. Multiple unconditional outgoing flows: invalid; engine confused. No default flow: incident risk. For the scenario (two conditional flows from Activity): Valid BPMN. Implicit XOR-like behavior. Better with explicit Gateway: clarity.

findings.message: option text contains its own verdict/rationale
